solution: 
```java
class Solution {
    public boolean uniqueOccurrences(int[] arr) {
        Map<Integer, Integer> countMap = new HashMap<>();
        Set<Integer> occurrenceSet = new HashSet<>();

        for (int num : arr) {
            countMap.put(num, countMap.getOrDefault(num, 0) + 1);
        }

        for (int count : countMap.values()) {
            if (!occurrenceSet.add(count)) {
                return false;
            }
        }

        return true;
    }
}
```
efficiency: HIGH
time complexity: O(n)
space complexity: O(n)
energy implications: LOW
explanation: <The solution uses a HashMap to count occurrences of each number and a HashSet to ensure uniqueness of these counts. It efficiently checks for uniqueness by attempting to add each count to the set, returning false if a duplicate is found. The approach is optimal for the problem constraints.>
